title: Ritsukos level of secret knowledge
wikitext:
[]: Ritsuko's level of secret knowledge             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Jul 19, 2008 3:54 am (Edit: removed a factual error. Also, I've since cut down my thesis from "Ritsuko might not have known about Lilith" to "Ritsuko might not have known that Rei is made from Lilith", but I leave this post otherwise intact for the sake of record.) Continuing this discussion here since it was off-topic in the other topic. Here's a condensed version of a couple of things I've found indicating Ritsuko may not have known about Lilith in ep23. They're only indicators, but proof is hard to come by in NGE. For the record, I'm not a diehard fanatic of this theory, it's just what makes sense to me until something else makes more sense. 1: Ritsuko never demonstrates knowledge of Eva-01's uniqueness; sometimes her behavior even hints otherwise. In ep16, when Eva-01 rips open Leliel, Ritsuko is horrified along with everyone else and says "What kind... what kind of monster have we copied from?" Note she does not say "What kind of thing have we created?" She invokes its substrate specifically , with horror. Lilith has been exclusively docile. Adam is the one who went out of control and nearly blew up the world, spawned the Angels, etc. This line makes a lot more sense if she thinks Eva-01 is made from Adam like all the others. That would also put it in line with her theme of thinking of the Evas as man's (perhaps foolish) attempt to resurrect the god they were punished for trying to claim as their own, as things they maybe shouldn't be toying with. 2: Ritsuko is shocked by Eva-01 eating an S2. In ep19, Eva-01 eats the S2 and breaks from its bindings. We find out this was apparently part of Gendo's script all along, which Fuyutsuki and even Kaji seem to understand. But not Ritsuko. She goes "masaka?!" like everyone else. If Ritsuko was really as in-the-know as GenFu's level, you'd expect she'd have a similar "A-ha!" But she doesn't. And she never appears to become informed that it was part of the plan. For that matter, Ritsuko goes "masaka?!" along with everyone else every time an Eva goes berserk in battle, while GenFu rarely show concern. They always seem to know something she doesn't. Thematically, she always seems to be in the pit alongside the other groundlings, with perhaps only a slightly better idea of what's going on. This also fits with her theme of being lower on the totem pole than she'd like to think. --------------------------------------- And unless you try to shit us of her not knowing about Adam sample and all relevant things like that there's no way she would make the Lilith=Adam misconception...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jul 19, 2008 1:23 pm From the other thread, because it's still relevant: I wrote: She knows it has to start there... but that doesn't necessarily mean she knows what is really going on. Knowing about the embryo doesn't mean she knows that the TD giant isn't just a copy made from that sample. As far as she knows it could just be 'Arctic God' > sample and 'Adam' > Rei clones and Shogoki. If she does know the full truth I think it's quite likely that she only learned it during her abduction/rape/interrogation scene with Seele. Only recently coming into the knowledge of Gendo's true aim would go a long way towards explaining her sudden meltdown and hostility to Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Jul 19, 2008 9:31 pm I think Ritsuko pretty much knows as much as Fuyutsuki does; there is the overriding question of "how much about Nerv's ultimate goal actually being Gendo's private drive to get back with Yui" did other staff members know about. Still, in public when she goes "that just can't happen" its either because honestly no one not even Gendo knows, or she's kind of faking to seem like she doesn't know as much as she does.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Jul 19, 2008 10:22 pm V wrote: I think Ritsuko pretty much knows as much as Fuyutsuki does; there is the overriding question of "how much about Nerv's ultimate goal actually being Gendo's private drive to get back with Yui" did other staff members know about. Still, in public when she goes "that just can't happen" its either because honestly no one not even Gendo knows, or she's kind of faking to seem like she doesn't know as much as she does.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Jul 20, 2008 9:14 am Monk Ed wrote: Zuggy wrote: Huh? I've never understood the confusion over what Ritsuko says in #23.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jul 20, 2008 8:04 pm But not a single mention is made of finding a second 'god' in Lilith, where Rei's pattern came from, how they got the souls for the evas, or even what the whole point of this project was going to be when the angels were dealt with. Thus I conclude that even at this late point she might not truely know what is going on. If she knew what point would there be in hiding that information now?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jul 21, 2008 12:03 am I already know that interpretation, Zuggy. The problem is that while each part of it makes sense on its own, as a whole it's aimless. Each part doesn't build properly on the last towards its point (justification to destroy dummies), according to what I know of Japanese composition. Below is how I read it. IIRC from class, Japanese composition goes: subject -> insert surrounding details -> end with how details relate back to subject, much like its sentence structure. Also keep in mind that it's an extremely context-sensitive language; each new sentence must be read taking into consideration the sentences said before it. [code:1]RITSUKO (OFF): These are just dummies. And nothing more than parts for Rei.[/code:1] She introduces her subject: the dummies. What follows, then, should be an explanation as to why they're "just dummies". [code:1]Man found God and in their joy, tried to make Him theirs. That's why there was divine retribution. That was fifteen years ago. The God they found had also disappeared by then. But then, they tried to resurrect God on their own. The result was Adam. And imitating God, they created people from Adam. The result was the Evas. [/code:1] First, she defines her terms. It seems that to define the dummies she feels she must start with defining Adam, then use Adam to define Eva. By the end of the above segment she has defined Eva as "humans made from Adam." [code:1] Shinji (OFF): People? They're human? RITSUKO: Yes, they're human. The Evas do not intrinsically have souls, but they have human souls embedded in them. They were all salvaged. [/code:1] She explains why she calls them human, in turn expanding her definition of Eva to "humans made from Adam, which were not born with souls, but are still human because they have salvaged human souls embedded in them." [code:1] The only vessel that contained a soul was Rei. She was the only one born with a soul. [/code:1] Using Evas as a reference point (remember context), she now defines Rei as distinguished from them by the fact that she was born with a soul. In other words, she's saying "Rei is a human made from Adam, who unlike the Evas was born with a soul, (and that soul makes her human)." One might argue that the only similarity Ritsuko means to imply is that they're created humans. But if that were true, she would've started with Lilith rather than bothering to bring up Adam at all, or wouldn't have bothered to bring up either. [code:1] The Chamber of Gaf was empty, you see. [/code:1] She inserts a brief footnote on why Rei was born with a soul but the Evas were not: the Chamber of Guf was empty. Japanese composition prefers subject - object - relation; the dummies have not been defined yet so there is no way this statement is meant to explain the difference between Rei and the dummies. It's almost certainly to explain the difference between Rei and the Evas . I'm not sure but I think your interpretation sees it this way too, so it's not a big point of contention. (Besides, why would it matter that the clones don't have souls because Guf was empty? If a soul of any old pilot would do they could harvest them as easily as they harvest mothers' souls. The point of the dummy system is that it's not a regular human soul, with its own will -- it's an obedient machine soul construct they can control. We already know, from the Evas' cores, just how unruly a regular human soul is.) [code:1] These things here that look like Rei have no souls. They're just vessels. That's why I'm going to destroy them. Because I hate them. [/code:1] And now the point, where she relates all of this back to the subject: she uses Rei as a reference point to define the dummies, by saying they differ in that they have no souls at all. And that's why it's okay for her to kill them for no greater reason than hatred: because they're not human, due to lack of souls. Reading it this way, each part builds upon the last and serves a purpose towards explaining the end point: Why it's okay for me to kill these things . Its only reliance is that Ritsuko thinks Rei is made from Adam. When we also consider that she doesn't seem to recognize that Eva-01 is made from Lilith (ep16), and that Lilith isn't even introduced until the next episode, and that Gendo would have plenty of reasons not to tell Ritsuko the full truth of Rei's origin, then I wouldn't say it's far-fetched at all that we're supposed to read it that way. And if we're meant to read it that way at this point and nothing later contradicts that she doesn't know Rei is made from Lilith, is it not rational to believe it could actually be true?

[]: Re: Ritsuko's level of secret knowledge             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 21, 2008 2:36 am Monk Ed wrote: Less notably, in ep20 she refers to Eva-01 as "not just an Adam copy, because it's imbued with a human will" (the subtitles omit but you can clearly hear her say "Adamu copy"), although it's to Misato and lying to her is nothing new.

[]: Re: Ritsuko's level of secret knowledge             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jul 21, 2008 3:00 am Reichu wrote: Monk Ed wrote: Less notably, in ep20 she refers to Eva-01 as "not just an Adam copy, because it's imbued with a human will" (the subtitles omit but you can clearly hear her say "Adamu copy"), although it's to Misato and lying to her is nothing new.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Fri Jul 25, 2008 3:21 am Based on my knowladge Ritsuko has among one of the highest clearances of all NERV personell. Although she has access to the information it appears she is under a need to know term and therefore only knows the stuff she needs to for her job. Relating to what the angles are capable of my guess is that she just seems a little more startled than everybody else

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 25, 2008 12:33 pm I still say she can't possibly know everything... at least, not until ep23. If she did know about Gendo's plan to rescue Yui then why it the world would she possibly stay with him for so long? Remember that Ritz didn't join until after Yui was gone and that her mother only recognized Rei's identity right before her own death, not having a chance to share this info. She may well have known about instrumentality, or at least the Seele version of that plan, but I don't for a second believe she knew the full story. Undoubtedly Seele fed her a completely new line when she was turned by them against Gendo, but her speech makes it sound like she still isn't fully aware of the situation.

Japanese composition prefers subject - object - relation; the dummies have not been defined yet so there is no way this statement is meant to explain the difference between Rei and the dummies. It's almost certainly to explain the difference between Rei and the Evas . I'm not sure but I think your interpretation sees it this way too, so it's not a big point of contention. (Besides, why would it matter that the clones don't have souls because Guf was empty? If a soul of any old pilot would do they could harvest them as easily as they harvest mothers' souls. The point of the dummy system is that it's not a regular human soul, with its own will -- it's an obedient machine soul construct they can control. Reading it this way, each part builds upon the last and serves a purpose towards explaining the end point: Why it's okay for me to kill these things . Its only reliance is that Ritsuko thinks Rei is made from Adam. When we also consider that she doesn't seem to recognize that Eva-01 is made from Lilith (ep16), and that Lilith isn't even introduced until the next episode, and that Gendo would have plenty of reasons not to tell Ritsuko the full truth of Rei's origin, then I wouldn't say it's far-fetched at all that we're supposed to read it that way. And if we're meant to read it that way at this point and nothing later contradicts that she doesn't know Rei is made from Lilith, is it not rational to believe it could actually be true?
